detective_persona.name: Det. Sarah Okafor
detective_persona.annotation_voice: Terse, time-obsessed, and systems-minded. Writes in clipped fragments, uses arrows to connect timestamps, and boxes anything that needs a cross-check.

A1.emotional_beat: The spectacle curdles into dread as a viral event becomes a real death with a hard timestamp.

detective_annotations.content: Hinge time: 2:47 AM — build the whole timeline around this.

A1.reading_order_note: Read first to lock the public timeline; everything else either tightens or contradicts it.

A2.emotional_beat: Names become suspects; the case feels suddenly smaller and more personal when everyone has a face.

detective_annotations.content: Make a grid: ACCESS / MOTIVE / OPPORTUNITY. Update after each doc.
detective_annotations.style_hint: square digital sticky with bullet points

A3.emotional_beat: A chill hits when the ‘shadow’ appears—proof the stream didn’t just fail; someone was present.

detective_annotations.content: Shadow = second person. Don’t guess who yet.
Where could this doorway be from? Need floor plan later.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin arrow drawn to margin note

A4.emotional_beat: Relief turns uneasy: the receipt looks like an alibi, but the missing address feels intentional.

detective_annotations.content: 2:51 AM — only 4 min after cutout. Too tight to ignore.
Address cropped… why? Need FULL delivery location.

A5.emotional_beat: The fan energy turns predatory when you realize he’s close enough to watch the building, not just the feed.

detective_annotations.content: Geo tag near studio. Proximity ≠ entry. Verify with access logs.
(Maybe he got inside via a side door?) — confirm/deny.
detective_annotations.style_hint: this note is intentionally WRONG/biased to mimic detective overreach

B1.emotional_beat: A money motive feels plausible, but the ‘no contact’ line lands like a rehearsed corporate script.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Haven’t spoken’ — subpoena call/SMS to verify.

B1.reading_order_note: Keep Jordan’s exact phrasing in mind; C2 is designed to confirm the lie but soften motive.

B2.emotional_beat: Grief becomes defensive when the drinks come up—the same object that comforts him becomes the weapon.

detective_annotations.content: Drink chain starts here. Track: bought → stored → delivered → accessed.
Specific detail (HEB / lane) sounds like real memory… but conflicts w/ ‘right before’ claim.

B3.emotional_beat: His calm composure feels helpful—until it reads like performance, not shock.

detective_annotations.content: His entire story rests on WHERE the delivery went. Wait for full address.

B4.emotional_beat: The case turns cold and undeniable: this wasn’t fate, it was chemistry.

detective_annotations.content: Stimulant + energy drink = deliberate tampering.
Who could access the drinks AFTER drop-off? (Control room? fridge?)

B5.emotional_beat: Ty’s eagerness curdles into panic; he sounds guilty until you notice he keeps dodging ‘how would I get in?’

detective_annotations.content: Lock his claim: ‘never entered.’ This is testable w/ building logs.

B5.reading_order_note: This is meant to feel damning until C1 cleanly disproves entry.

C1.emotional_beat: The boring truth hits hard: the most suspicious person can’t be the killer because he couldn’t even get inside.

detective_annotations.content: NO entry for Riggs. Cross him off as primary actor.
So who DOES have late-night access? Look for 2:15–2:50 window.

C2.emotional_beat: Jordan’s lie is confirmed, but the tone is resignation—not menace—making ‘motive’ feel less lethal.

detective_annotations.content: 12-min call exists → he lied. Now read the *content* for intent.
‘I’m out’ reads like closure. Motive weakens here.

C3.emotional_beat: The stomach-drop pivot: the alibi becomes a location pin at the crime scene.

detective_annotations.content: CONTROL ROOM SIDE ENTRANCE — this is the alibi collapse.
2:47 cutout → 2:51 delivery. Four minutes = next door.

C4.emotional_beat: The ‘how’ snaps into place: the control room isn’t adjacent—it’s a hidden route that explains the shadow.

detective_annotations.content: Connecting door = silent movement. Shadow explained.
Trace path: side entrance → control room → door → studio → back.

R1.emotional_beat: A suspect becomes human again: the paperwork finally grants Chloe the relief she couldn’t argue into existence.

detective_annotations.content: Chloe CLEARED. Keep: she delivered drinks, but no access during window.

R2.emotional_beat: The revenge story deflates: anger existed, but the evidence pushes it back into civil court where it belongs.

detective_annotations.content: Motive-only suspect. Without access/opportunity, it doesn’t carry.

R3.emotional_beat: Creepiness is separated from culpability—your instincts get corrected by hard access proof.

detective_annotations.content: Reminder: ‘Weird’ ≠ ‘did it.’ Evidence first.

R4.emotional_beat: The story turns prosecutable: betrayal becomes a charge backed by timestamps, space, and method.

detective_annotations.content: Probable cause chain: A3 + C3 + C4 = presence + path + opportunity.
detective_annotations.style_hint: circle with three doc IDs written inside

detective_annotations.content: Keep it clean in court: location + timing + means.

pacing_warnings: Envelope C reads very fast by minimum-word estimates; ensure production text and annotations make it feel like a decisive ‘pivot’ sequence, not a brief administrative interlude.
CONTRA-05 spans 10 documents (A3→C4). Consider stronger ‘bookmark’ reminders in A3/B docs so the shadow stays cognitively active.

trojan_horse_verification.appears_as: A routine food-delivery receipt screenshot that seems like an alibi.
trojan_horse_verification.actually_proves: Sets up the later location reveal; when paired with C3 it places Sam at the control room side entrance during the murder window.

trojan_horse_verification.appears_as: A mundane customer-support email thread about a delivery problem.
trojan_horse_verification.actually_proves: Provides the complete address (‘Control Room Side Entrance’), breaking Sam’s claimed-at-home alibi and anchoring the pivot.
